About Me

Engineering Safer Slopes,
One Analysis at a Time

Final-year Civil Engineering student at National College of Engineering (NCE), Tribhuvan University, with a strong interest in geotechnical and slope engineering.

Experienced in academic project work on slope stability analysis of natural rock slopes using the Limit Equilibrium Method (LEM), focusing on failure mechanisms and stability behavior in hilly terrain conditions.

Seeking an entry-level opportunity to apply technical knowledge in geotechnical engineering and gain practical field experience in real-world engineering projects.

Final Year Project

Featured Work

01
Evaluation and Stability Assessment of Natural Rock Slope at Chainage 10+500 Along Tribhuvan Highway, Kathmandu
Supervisor: Er. Arjun Poudel, KEC | 2082

Conducted rock mass classification of phyllite slope (RMR = 61, GSI = 50) and kinematic analysis using Rocscience DIPS 6.0. Performed slope stability analysis using Bishop, Janbu, and Spencer methods under static and seismic conditions. Identified critical wedge failure and improved factor of safety from 0.845 to 1.48 using rock bolt stabilization. Prepared a comprehensive 149-page technical report with stabilization recommendations.

Rocscience SLIDE DIPS 6.0 LEM Kinematic Analysis Rock Bolt Stabilization

Work Gallery

Site & Project Visuals

Project Visual
Field Survey — Chainage 10+500
Total station survey at the project site along Tribhuvan Highway, Kathmandu
Project Visual
Satellite Site Location Map
Google Earth aerial view of the rock slope at Chainage 10+500, Tribhuvan Highway
Project Visual
Kinematic Analysis — Critical Wedge Failure
Stereonet (equal angle, lower hemisphere) showing planar sliding kinematic analysis using Rocscience DIPS 6.0. Slope dip 46°/356°, friction angle 30°, lateral limits ±20°. 1 of 4 joint sets critical for planar sliding (25%).
Project Visual
GSI Sensitivity Analysis
Factor of Safety variation across Bishop, Janbu and Spencer methods under three GSI scenarios. Series1 (GSI=60) yields FOS up to 4.10 (Spencer), confirming stable conditions post-reinforcement.
Project Visual
3D Wedge Analysis — Rock Bolt Stabilisation
SWedge 3D perspective view of the critical wedge (FOS = 0.845 → 1.48 post rock bolt). Cyan vectors show bolt force distribution; magenta arrow indicates sliding direction.
Project Visual
Taking Survey Data
Field data collection using survey equipment at project site
Project Visual
Rocscience SLIDE Work
Slope stability analysis using Rocscience SLIDE — Spencer method, FS: 2.505
